title: One Last Page, Stuck to the Back Cover

targetWords.zh: 他們（他們，他們的，他們的，他們自己）

sentences.en: I found one more blank page stuck to the back cover of my notebook, so here are a few tiny things before summer truly begins.
sentences.zh: 我在筆記本封底發現還黏著一頁空白頁，所以在暑假真正開始之前，再補幾件小小的事。

sentences.en: William and his rat already look alike somehow, both a little round and both super curious about absolutely everything.
sentences.zh: 不知怎麼地，William 跟他的老鼠已經有點像了，兩個都圓圓的，也都對什麼都超級好奇。

sentences.en: Sophia told a story about her grandmother's old coal stove, which took forever to heat but somehow made the best soup she ever tasted.
sentences.zh: Sophia 說了一個她祖母老煤爐的故事，那個爐子加熱慢到不行，但煮出來的湯不知怎麼是她吃過最好喝的。

sentences.en: On the pet adoption form, one line simply asked for the rat's sex, and William proudly wrote "female" in his very best handwriting.
sentences.zh: 領養表格上有一行單純問老鼠的性別，William 用他最漂亮的字驕傲地寫下「母的」。

sentences.en: We stopped at the drug store on the way home, since Mom needed a small tube of cream for a scrape on her hip from gardening.
sentences.zh: 回家路上我們在藥局停了一下，因為媽媽在花園弄傷了髖部，需要一小條藥膏。

sentences.en: The pharmacist there, an older woman everyone calls Mrs. Ho, measured out exactly one inch of cream onto a small sample card for Mom to try first.
sentences.zh: 藥局裡的藥師，一位大家都叫她何太太的年長女士，量了整整一英吋的藥膏在小樣品卡上讓媽媽先試用。

sentences.en: I asked Mom if the cream would work, and she said, quite simply, "Nope, probably not, but it is worth a try anyway."
sentences.zh: 我問媽媽這個藥膏有沒有用，她很簡單地說：「大概沒用，但還是值得試試看。」

sentences.en: William said his internal clock, whatever that means to a seven-year-old, was already telling him it was time for a summer snack.
sentences.zh: William 說他的生理時鐘，雖然不知道對七歲小孩來說這代表什麼，已經在告訴他該吃暑假點心了。

sentences.en: In relation to everything that happened this whole school year, one small snack request felt like a perfectly reasonable way to end the day.
sentences.zh: 跟這整個學年發生的所有事情比起來，一個小小的點心要求感覺是結束這一天再合理不過的方式了。

sentences.en: Bernice and I agreed that whatever adventures come next, we would write about them for each other, and for anyone else, wherever they might be.
sentences.zh: 我跟 Bernice 說好，不管接下來會發生什麼冒險，我們都會寫下來給彼此看，也給不管在哪裡的其他人看。

sentences.en: Perry, curled up beside the notebook the whole time, seemed perfectly content just being included in every single page.
sentences.zh: 全程蜷在筆記本旁邊的 Perry，看起來很滿足自己能出現在每一頁裡。

sentences.en: William and his rat both fell asleep on the couch, each one apparently pleased with themselves for no clear reason at all.
sentences.zh: William 跟他的老鼠都在沙發上睡著了，兩個看起來都莫名地對自己感到很滿意，也不知道到底是為了什麼。

sentences.en: And with that, my very first diary is officially, completely, finally full, from cover to cover, in a way I am genuinely proud of.
sentences.zh: 就這樣，我人生第一本日記正式地、完全地、終於地寫滿了，從封面到封底，我真的為此感到驕傲。

quiz.question: What does William write for the rat's sex on the adoption form?
What does Mom say when Coco asks if the cream will work?
options: "Nope, probably not, but it is worth a try anyway."
"I don't know what this cream is."

quiz.question: How does Coco describe her notebook at the very end?
options: Officially, completely, finally full, from cover to cover.
